claim: JCVI-Syn3A has approximately 187 RNA polymerase (RNAP) holoenzymes per cell.
source_detail: Thornburg 2026 reports 187 RNA polymerases per cell as a measured input parameter (sourced from Bianchi 2022 quantitative proteomics of Syn3A). Their 4DWCM spatial simulation additionally predicts that ~34%% of those 187 RNAP are actively transcribing on average early in the cell cycle, with the rest diffusing on/off genes.
caveats: Single-cell proteomics has measurement noise; per-cell count varies across replicates.
'RNA polymerase' here is the holoenzyme - alpha2 * beta * beta' * sigma. Counting by sigma factor would give a different number.
Extracted from WebSearch snippets of the Thornburg 2026 paper in Session 11; the full paper's supplementary tables will have tighter error bars.
